SEARCH
Package basicFiles.files

Class TextFile

java.lang.Object
basicFiles.files.BaseFile
basicFiles.files.TextFile
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<String>
public class TextFile extends BaseFile implements Iterable<String>
Concrete class representing a text file (typically .txt). It allows for reading and writing strings line by line while maintaining an in-memory representation using a LinkedList.

This class implements Iterable, enabling standard for-each loops:
for (String line : myFile) {...}

Author:
Elias Kassas

    Constructors

    Description

    Default constructor for a standard .txt file.
    TextFile(File folder, String fileName, String extension)
    Constructor for a text file with a dynamic extension.
    TextFile(String fileName)
    Constructor for a text file located in the current working directory.

    Adds a single line to the in-memory buffer without immediate disk synchronization.

  • Constructor Details

    • TextFile

      public TextFile(File folder, String fileName, String extension) throws IOException
      Constructor for a text file with a dynamic extension. The file is automatically loaded into memory if it exists; otherwise, it is created.
      Parameters:
      folder - The folder containing the file.
      fileName - The name of the file without its extension.
      extension - The specific file extension (e.g., ".txt", ".log").
      Throws:
      IOException - If the file cannot be accessed or read.

    • TextFile

      public TextFile(File folder, String fileName) throws IOException
      Default constructor for a standard .txt file.
      Parameters:
      folder - The directory containing the file.
      fileName - The name of the file without extension.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a reading error occurs.

    • TextFile

      public TextFile(String fileName) throws IOException
      Constructor for a text file located in the current working directory.
      Parameters:
      fileName - The file name.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a reading error occurs.

  • Method Details

    • read

      public void read() throws IOException
      Reads the file content and populates the in-memory line list. Updates the internal modification timestamp upon successful completion.
      Specified by:
      read in class BaseFile
      Throws:
      IOException - If the file is missing or permissions are insufficient.

    • write

      public void write(Object... args) throws IOException
      Replaces the current file content with the provided arguments and writes to disk. This method utilizes a write lock to ensure thread safety during the overwrite.
      Specified by:
      write in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Objects or Lists of objects whose toString() values will form the new lines.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a writing error occurs.

    • append

      public void append(Object... args) throws IOException
      Adds new lines to the end of the existing content and synchronizes with the disk.
      Specified by:
      append in class BaseFile
      Parameters:
      args - Objects or Lists of objects to append.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the write operation fails.

    • add

      public void add(Object o)
      Adds a single line to the in-memory buffer without immediate disk synchronization.
      Parameters:
      o - The object to add as a new line.

    • remove

      public void remove(Object o)
      Removes matching lines from the in-memory buffer based on string equality.
      Parameters:
      o - The object representation to remove.

    • clear

      public void clear() throws IOException
      Erases all content from both the memory buffer and the physical file.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the file cannot be updated.

    • getContent

      public ArrayList<Object> getContent() throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Retrieves a copy of the lines currently held in memory. Ensures the buffer is synchronized with disk changes before returning.
      Returns:
      An ArrayList containing the file lines.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a synchronization read fails.
      ClassNotFoundException - If class resolution fails during read.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty() throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Checks if the line list is empty after ensuring data is up to date.
      Returns:
      true if empty; false otherwise.
      Throws:
      IOException
      ClassNotFoundException

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Object o) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Performs a linear search for a line matching the string representation of an object.
      Parameters:
      o - The object to find.
      Returns:
      true if a match exists.
      Throws:
      IOException
      ClassNotFoundException

    • deletePhysicalFile

      public boolean deletePhysicalFile()
      Description copied from class: BaseFile
      Physically deletes the file from the file system.
      Specified by:
      deletePhysicalFile in class BaseFile
      Returns:
      true if the file was successfully deleted; false otherwise.

    • iterator

      public Iterator<String> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the lines of the file. Updates the internal buffer from disk before creating the iterator.
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<String>
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable iterator of file lines.

    • getLine

      public String getLine(int line) throws ClassNotFoundException, IOException
      Retrieves a specific line by its index.
      Parameters:
      line - The zero-based line index.
      Returns:
      The content of the line.
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException
      IOException

    • setLine

      public boolean setLine(int line, String newLine)
      Updates a specific line in the in-memory buffer. Note: This does not trigger an automatic write to disk.
      Parameters:
      line - The index to update.
      newLine - The new text content.
      Returns:
      Always true upon successful update.

    • getLineCount

      public int getLineCount() throws ClassNotFoundException, IOException
      Returns the total number of lines in the file.
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException
      IOException
